minimum_specs_note: Sygen needs at least 2 vCPU, 2 GB RAM, 20 GB disk to run core + admin + updater containers. 4 GB RAM recommended if you plan to run sub-agents. Each provider entry below shows the smallest plan that meets these minima.

providers.name: Hetzner Cloud
providers.description_long: European cloud provider known for ridiculously low prices and rock-solid uptime. Great default pick for budget-conscious users in EU and US.

providers.name: DigitalOcean
providers.tagline: Popular global VPS with simple UI
providers.description_long: Developer-friendly cloud with droplets in 14+ regions worldwide, transparent pricing and great docs. Good middle-ground between budget and managed clouds.

providers.name: Vultr
providers.description_long: Hourly billing, 30+ data centers worldwide including Tokyo, Sydney, São Paulo, Johannesburg. Layer 3/4/7 DDoS protection included. Strong picks for niche regions.

providers.name: Linode (Akamai)
providers.description_long: Linode is now part of Akamai with global edge network. Reliable performance, predictable pricing, mature platform. Good for production workloads.

providers.name: OVHcloud
providers.description_long: Europe's largest cloud provider with strong DDoS protection (industry-leading) and data centers across France, Germany, UK, Poland, Canada, US, Singapore, Australia.

providers.name: Contabo
providers.description_long: German budget cloud with extremely generous resources for the price. EU + US + Asia data centers. Free DDoS protection. Slower setup than Hetzner but cheaper for higher tiers.

providers.name: Scaleway
providers.description_long: French cloud provider with strong EU sovereignty story. Hourly billing, ARM and AMD instance types, fast NVMe storage. Good for EU developers who care about data residency.

providers.name: UpCloud
providers.description_long: Finnish premium cloud known for fastest disk IO in benchmarks (MaxIOPS storage). 100% uptime SLA. Pricier than Hetzner but rock-solid.

providers.name: IONOS Cloud
providers.description_long: Established German hosting brand (formerly 1&1). GDPR-compliant data centers across Germany, France, UK, Spain, US. Promo intro pricing, then €5+/mo.

providers.name: netcup
providers.description_long: Long-established German provider (since 2003) with Nuremberg data center. Excellent price-to-performance, often used for self-hosting and dev.

providers.name: Aruba Cloud
providers.description_long: Italy's largest hosting provider with data centers in Italy, Czech Republic, France, Germany, UK. Strong choice for Southern Europe.

providers.name: AWS Lightsail
providers.tagline: Simplified AWS for VPS use cases
providers.description_long: Amazon's simplified VPS offering. Flat pricing, AWS Shield Standard included, available in all major AWS regions worldwide. Use 2GB+ plan for Sygen.

providers.name: Hostinger VPS
providers.tagline: Budget VPS with annual contract discounts
providers.description_long: Lithuania-based mass-market hosting brand. KVM VPS in 7+ regions including India, Brazil, Singapore. Cheapest with annual contract; renewal price ~2x intro.

providers.name: Kamatera
providers.description_long: Israel-headquartered cloud provider with data centers across US, EU, Israel, Hong Kong, Canada. Pay-per-hour, fully customizable specs.

providers.name: RackNerd
providers.tagline: US budget VPS, KVM annual deals
providers.description_long: US budget VPS provider with recurring annual KVM deals. Mixed support reviews on Reddit, but many long-running customers. Good for hobby projects.

providers.name: InterServer
providers.description_long: Long-running US provider with price-lock guarantee (your renewal price never goes up). Multiple data centers in NJ, TX, CA. Solid for US workloads.

providers.name: Namecheap VPS
providers.description_long: Mostly known for domain registration; their VPS line is acceptable for basic workloads. Mixed reviews on support.

providers.name: Time4VPS
providers.description_long: Lithuanian provider with very competitive pricing. Single data center in Vilnius. Good for EU-East users on a budget.

providers.name: Atman
providers.description_long: Established Polish data center operator (Atman). Tier III+ infrastructure, strong reputation in CEE enterprise market.

providers.name: HostiQ
providers.description_long: One of Ukraine's largest hosters. Data centers in Kyiv plus EU mirror. Russian-language support and UAH pricing.

providers.name: HyperHost
providers.description_long: Ukrainian provider with data centers in Kyiv and Odessa plus EU mirrors. Good local choice for UA users; English UI available.

providers.name: Cityhost
providers.description_long: Ukrainian hoster with own DC in Kyiv. NVMe storage, 24/7 support in UA/RU/EN. Solid for users who want a local Ukrainian provider.

providers.name: ITL
providers.description_long: Ukrainian hoster with own infrastructure plus presence in 9 EU data centers. Migration assistance, 24/7 support.

providers.name: Selectel
providers.description_long: Major Russian cloud provider with data centers in Moscow, St. Petersburg, Novosibirsk. Crypto payments accepted. Tier III+ DCs.

providers.name: Yandex Cloud
providers.description_long: Russia's largest internet company cloud platform. Three availability zones in Russia. Strong for low-latency RU/BY/CIS workloads.

providers.name: Timeweb
providers.description_long: Russian provider with St. Petersburg, Moscow, Almaty (KZ), Amsterdam DCs. NVMe SSD, 24/7 RU support, top-rated on hostings.info.

providers.name: Reg.ru
providers.description_long: Russia's largest domain registrar. VPS line with Moscow + St. Petersburg DCs. Russian-language UI and support.

providers.name: RuVDS
providers.description_long: Russian provider with multi-region presence (Moscow, Switzerland, Netherlands, UK, Germany). Crypto payments. 99.999% uptime SLA claim.

providers.name: Beget
providers.description_long: Long-running Russian hoster with strong shared hosting heritage. Moscow data center. Friendly UI with Russian-language docs.

providers.name: FirstVDS
providers.description_long: Russian provider operating since 2002. Moscow + St. Petersburg DCs, transparent pricing, no overselling. Russian-language UI.

providers.name: VDSina
providers.description_long: Russian VDS with hourly billing, NVMe SSD, modern control panel. Moscow + Amsterdam + Frankfurt + USA DCs. Crypto + RUB payments.

providers.name: ConoHa VPS
providers.description_long: Japan's popular VPS service operated by GMO Internet. Tokyo + Osaka + Singapore DCs. Hourly billing, fast NVMe.

providers.name: Sakura Internet
providers.description_long: Long-running Japanese cloud (since 1996). Tokyo + Osaka + Ishikari (Hokkaido) DCs. Strong reputation, Japanese-language UI primary.

providers.name: Alibaba Cloud (ECS)
providers.description_long: China's largest cloud. Strong APAC + Middle East presence (Singapore, Hong Kong, Tokyo, Mumbai, Dubai, Jakarta). Free trial credits available.

providers.name: Tencent Cloud
providers.description_long: Tencent's international cloud with strong East Asia presence. Hong Kong, Singapore, Tokyo, Seoul, Mumbai, Bangkok. Aggressive intro pricing.

providers.name: BinaryLane
providers.tagline: Australian VPS, low ping for AU
providers.description_long: Australian-owned provider with Sydney + Melbourne + Brisbane + Perth + Adelaide DCs. Best low-latency option for AU/NZ users.

providers.name: VentraIP
providers.description_long: 100% Australian-owned with Sydney + Melbourne DCs. Strong Trustpilot reviews from AU users. Local 24/7 support.

providers.name: SiteHost
providers.description_long: Auckland-based provider, NZ's most established cloud host. Local NZ support and pricing in NZD. Good for low-latency NZ users.

providers.name: Locaweb
providers.description_long: Brazil's biggest hosting company. São Paulo DCs, Portuguese support. Solid for LATAM users wanting local payment options (boleto, PIX).

providers.name: KingHost
providers.description_long: Brazilian provider with Porto Alegre + São Paulo presence. Portuguese-language UI and PIX/boleto payments.

providers.name: MilesWeb
providers.description_long: Pune-based Indian provider with 32 global DC partner locations. INR pricing, Indian-language support. Good entry point for Indian users.

providers.name: HostMyCode
providers.description_long: Indian provider with Noida DC plus EU mirror. AMD Ryzen + NVMe, LiteSpeed-optimized. INR pricing.

providers.name: LightNode
providers.description_long: Edge VPS provider with rare locations: Dubai, Bahrain, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Cairo, Lagos, Tehran adjacents. Hourly billing.

providers.name: Natro
providers.description_long: Largest Turkish hosting brand. Istanbul DC, Turkish-language UI, TL/USD pricing. Best low-latency for Turkey users.

providers.name: Xneelo
providers.description_long: Formerly Hetzner South Africa, rebranded to Xneelo in 2023. Cape Town + Johannesburg DCs, ZAR pricing, local 24/7 support.

providers.name: Afrihost
providers.description_long: Major South African ISP/host. Johannesburg + Cape Town DCs, ZAR pricing, local support hours align with SA timezone.

providers.name: HostAfrica
providers.description_long: South African hoster with multiple African regions: SA, Kenya, Ghana, Nigeria. KVM virt, 1 Gbps unmetered, 99.9% SLA.

providers.name: WEDOS
providers.description_long: Czech Republic's largest hosting brand (since 2009). Own data centers in Hluboká nad Vltavou with renewable energy. Strong choice for Czech and CEE users wanting local data residency.

providers.name: Forpsi
providers.description_long: Long-running Czech provider (now part of Italian INTERNET CZ group) with data centers in Prague and Lugano. Solid for CEE/IT users. CZK and EUR pricing.

providers.name: M247
providers.description_long: UK-headquartered network with deep Romanian operations and 60+ global PoPs. Solid backbone, good ME/SEE latency. Pricier than budget tier but bigger reach.

providers.name: SuperHosting.BG
providers.description_long: Bulgaria's biggest hosting brand. Own DCs in Sofia, Bulgarian-language UI, BGN pricing. Top pick for low-latency BG users.

providers.name: RackForest
providers.description_long: Hungarian provider with own Tier III DC in Budapest. Good local choice for HU users; English UI available, HUF + EUR pricing.

providers.name: nazwa.pl
providers.description_long: One of Poland's largest hosting brands. Krakow + Warsaw DCs, Polish-language UI, PLN pricing. Mass-market reliability for PL users.

providers.name: Hostings.ge
providers.description_long: Tbilisi-based provider — the most established Georgian hoster. Local DC in Georgia plus EU mirror. GEL/USD pricing, EN/KA/RU support.

providers.name: PS.kz
providers.description_long: Largest Kazakh registrar/host operating since 2003. Own DC in Almaty, KZT/USD pricing, RU/KZ/EN support. Best low-latency option for KZ users.

providers.name: Turhost
providers.description_long: Long-running Turkish provider (since 2001) with Istanbul DC. Turkish UI, TL/USD pricing. Good alternative to Natro for TR users.

providers.name: UOL Host
providers.description_long: Cloud arm of UOL (Brazil's biggest internet portal). São Paulo DC, Portuguese UI, PIX/boleto payments. Solid mainstream LATAM option.

providers.name: Donweb
providers.description_long: Argentine provider with own DC in Rosario and Buenos Aires presence. Spanish UI, ARS/USD pricing, local payment methods. Best low-latency for AR users.

providers.name: HostDime
providers.description_long: US-rooted provider with strong LATAM expansion: own DCs in Mexico, Brazil, Colombia plus India and US. Decent pick for cross-border LATAM workloads.

providers.name: Truehost
providers.description_long: Kenya-headquartered hoster with offices in Kenya, Nigeria, Uganda, Ghana, Rwanda. KSH/NGN/USD pricing, English/Swahili support.

providers.name: Web Africa
providers.description_long: Major South African ISP/host with Johannesburg + Cape Town DCs. ZAR pricing, local 24/7 support. Good Afrihost alternative.

providers.name: Vodien
providers.description_long: Singapore-headquartered provider with SG and AU DCs. SGD pricing, English support, popular with SE Asia SMBs and Australian users.

providers.name: Exabytes
providers.description_long: Largest Malaysian hosting brand with offices in MY, SG, ID, TH. Local-currency billing, EN/Bahasa support. Solid for ASEAN SMBs.

providers.name: Cafe24
providers.description_long: South Korea's largest hosting brand. Own DCs in Seoul, KRW pricing, Korean-first UI (limited English). Top pick for low-latency KR.

providers.name: Gabia
providers.description_long: Long-running Korean provider (KRX-listed). Seoul DC, KRW pricing, Korean-language UI. Solid Cafe24 alternative for KR users wanting different vendor.

providers.name: Vinahost
providers.description_long: Vietnamese provider with HCMC + Hanoi DCs. NVMe SSD, VND pricing, Vietnamese/English support. Best low-latency choice for VN users.

providers.name: HostingRaja
providers.description_long: Bengaluru-based Indian hoster. DCs in India + multiple global mirrors. INR pricing, English/Hindi/regional language support.

providers.name: Infomaniak
providers.description_long: Geneva-based Swiss provider operating own DCs in Switzerland exclusively. ISO 27001/14001 certified, 100% renewable energy, strong privacy stance. Premium choice for users who want Swiss jurisdiction.

providers.name: GleSYS
providers.description_long: Swedish provider with own DCs in Stockholm, Falkenberg, Helsinki, Amsterdam. SEK/EUR pricing, English/Swedish support. Strong choice for Nordic users.

providers.name: BuyVM
providers.description_long: Run by Frantech (BC, Canada) since 2010. KVM slices in Las Vegas, NYC, Miami, Luxembourg. Free DDoS filtering for $3/mo, network-attached block storage. Cult favorite on LowEndTalk.

providers.name: HostHatch
providers.description_long: Sweden-rooted provider with 30+ global locations including Tokyo, Hong Kong, Mumbai, São Paulo, Johannesburg. Famous for huge annual discounts on LowEndTalk. Mixed support reviews.
